questions.q: Who were the parents of Parikshit?
questions.explanation: Parikshit was the son of Abhimanyu and Uttara, born after his father had already fallen at Kurukshetra.

questions.q: Which weapon struck Parikshit while he was still in his mother's womb?
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama bent the Brahmastra into Uttara's womb to end the Pandava line, and the child was born lifeless.

questions.q: Who revived the stillborn Parikshit?
questions.explanation: Krishna brought the dead infant to life by an act of truth, counted among his last great deeds.

questions.q: Parikshit was the grandson of which Pandava?
questions.explanation: As the son of Abhimanyu, Parikshit was Arjuna's grandson and the sole heir of the Kuru line.

questions.q: When was Parikshit crowned king of Hastinapura?
options: Immediately after the Kurukshetra war ended
On the day Krishna died at Prabhasa
When Dhritarashtra retired to the forest
When the Pandavas departed on their final journey
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira installed Parikshit on the throne before the Pandavas renounced the world and walked north.

questions.q: Which serpent killed King Parikshit?
questions.explanation: Takshaka, king of the nagas, bit Parikshit and burned him with poison in fulfillment of a sage's curse.

questions.q: Who was Parikshit's son and successor on the throne?
questions.explanation: Janamejaya succeeded Parikshit and later held the great snake sacrifice to avenge him.

questions.q: Who loosed the weapon that struck Parikshit in the womb?
questions.explanation: Unable to withdraw his Brahmastra, Ashwatthama turned it on the unborn heir of the Pandavas.

questions.q: How many days did the curse give Parikshit to live?
questions.explanation: The curse decreed that within seven days the serpent Takshaka would burn the king with his venom.

questions.q: From which city did Parikshit rule the Kuru kingdom?
questions.explanation: Parikshit reigned from Hastinapura, the ancestral capital of the Kurus.

questions.q: By what means did Krishna revive the stillborn child of Uttara?
options: By invoking the Sanjivani lore of the gods
By transferring half of his own lifespan
By an act of truth, swearing on his own unbroken righteousness
By commanding the Brahmastra to return to Brahma
questions.explanation: Krishna staked the truth of his own virtues, declaring that if they were unbroken the child should live, and Parikshit breathed.

questions.q: What insult did Parikshit offer the meditating sage Shamika?
options: He draped a dead snake around the sage's neck
He overturned the sage's water pot
He struck the sage with the tip of his bow
He set fire to the sage's hermitage
questions.explanation: Angered by the sage's silence, the king lifted a dead snake with his bow and hung it on Shamika's shoulders.

questions.q: Who pronounced the curse of death upon Parikshit?
options: Shamika himself, roused from his trance
Takshaka, in the form of a brahmin
questions.explanation: Shringi, hearing of the insult to his father Shamika, cursed the king to die of Takshaka's bite within seven days.

questions.q: Why was Parikshit angry with the silent sage Shamika in the first place?
options: The sage had cursed the royal hunting party
Thirsty after a hunt, the king got no answer to his greeting and request for water
The sage refused to bless the royal heir
The sage had given shelter to the serpent Takshaka
questions.explanation: Exhausted and thirsty from chasing a deer, Parikshit took the sage's vow of silence for contempt.

questions.q: What protective measure did Parikshit take after learning of the curse?
options: He lived atop a guarded mansion raised on a single pillar
He hid in a mountain cave sealed with boulders
He sailed to an island free of serpents
He wore Ashwatthama's protective gem day and night
questions.explanation: The king moved to a closely guarded palace built on one column, surrounded by physicians and masters of mantras.

questions.q: How did Takshaka finally reach the well-guarded king?
options: He tunneled up through the pillar of the mansion
He took the form of a guard and entered at night
He hid as a tiny worm inside a fruit presented to the king
He summoned a storm that broke open the tower
questions.explanation: Takshaka became a small copper-colored worm inside a fruit carried in by disguised nagas, then sprang into his true form.

questions.q: Who was on his way to cure the king of any snakebite but turned back on the road?
options: The brahmin Kashyapa, master of poison cures
The sage Shamika, repenting his son's curse
questions.explanation: The brahmin Kashyapa could undo Takshaka's venom, but the serpent bribed him with wealth and he went home.

questions.q: What had the unborn Parikshit seen while the Brahmastra scorched his mother's womb?
options: His grandfather Arjuna shielding him with the Gandiva
His mother's prayers turning the weapon aside
The serpent Shesha coiled protectively around him
A radiant being of light turning the weapon away
questions.explanation: The child beheld a luminous figure, Krishna himself, warding off the weapon, and afterward sought that being in every man he met.

questions.q: How did the sage Shamika respond to his son's curse upon the king?
options: He doubled the curse for his son's sake
He disapproved and sent a disciple to warn the king
He demanded the king come and beg forgiveness
He withdrew into a vow of eternal silence
questions.explanation: Shamika rebuked Shringi for cursing a good king over a small offense and sent word so Parikshit could protect himself.

questions.q: Who served as the young Parikshit's teacher in arms and statecraft?
questions.explanation: Kripacharya, one of the seven immortals, lived on after the war to train Parikshit.

questions.q: Why was the child given the name Parikshit, in the epic's explanation?
options: He was born under a constellation called Parikshi
His golden birthmark was shaped like a crown
He was named for a sage who attended his birth
He was born as the perishing Kuru line was 'tested' to the brink of extinction
questions.explanation: The name recalls that he was born when the line had all but perished and was tested in the womb; he was also said to 'examine' all men for the being he saw there.

questions.q: Who was Parikshit's wife, the mother of Janamejaya?
questions.explanation: Parikshit married Madravati, and their eldest son Janamejaya inherited the throne.

questions.q: At what age did Parikshit die, as commonly computed from the epic?
questions.explanation: Born just after the war and crowned when the Pandavas left thirty-six years later, he reigned about twenty-four years and died at sixty.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana's telling, how did Parikshit spend his last seven days?
options: Performing a great fire sacrifice against serpents
Listening to the sage Shuka recite the Bhagavata on the Ganga's bank
Fasting in silence atop his pillared tower
Touring his kingdom to take leave of his people
questions.explanation: In the Bhagavata variant he renounced everything and heard Shuka narrate the Bhagavata; the Mahabharata instead has him guarded in the pillared mansion.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata tradition, where did Parikshit permit the spirit of Kali to dwell?
options: In gambling, drink, women, slaughter, and gold
In deserts, ruins, cremation grounds, and battlefields
In courts, markets, and the houses of the rich
In dreams, shadows, mirrors, and crossroads
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana tells how Parikshit subdued Kali and confined him to gambling, drinking, prostitution, slaughter, and gold.

questions.q: Who carried the sage Shamika's warning of the curse to King Parikshit?
questions.explanation: Shamika sent his gentle disciple Gaurmukha to tell the king of Shringi's curse so he could guard himself.

questions.q: How did Takshaka test the healing power of Kashyapa before bribing him to turn back?
options: He bit a cow, which Kashyapa restored to life
He bit a banyan tree to ashes, which Kashyapa revived
He poisoned a lake, which Kashyapa made pure again
He struck a guard dead, whom Kashyapa raised up
questions.explanation: Takshaka's bite burned a banyan to ashes, Kashyapa restored it whole, and the serpent then bought him off with greater wealth.

questions.q: On what truths did Krishna swear when he revived the dead infant Parikshit?
options: His love for the Pandavas and Draupadi
The merit of lifting Mount Govardhana
His vow to protect the lineage of Yadu
His never having spoken untruth nor fled from battle
questions.explanation: Krishna declared that as he had never uttered a falsehood even in jest and never turned back in battle, the child should live.

questions.q: Besides Janamejaya, which sons did Parikshit leave behind?
questions.explanation: The Adi Parva names Janamejaya's brothers as Shrutasena, Ugrasena, and Bhimasena, who attended his rites with him.

questions.q: In which book of the Mahabharata is the story of Parikshit's death told in full?
options: The Adi Parva, in the Astika cycle
questions.explanation: The curse, the tower, and Takshaka's bite are narrated in the Astika section of the Adi Parva, as prelude to the snake sacrifice.
